title: The Debt You Inherited
subtitle: Prosperity is fragile. Trust is precious.

turns.prompt: Hospital waiting lists hit record highs. Debt is already high. Everyone expects action.

choices.label: Borrow to fund hospitals

delayed.headline: Debt servicing starts to squeeze other budgets

choices.reaction: Waiting lists shrink. Bond markets watch closely.

choices.label: Raise taxes on high earners
choices.reaction: Public sector unions cautiously approve. Business lobbies fume.

choices.label: Cut military spending to fund health
choices.reaction: Military leaders warn of vulnerability. Health unions celebrate.

turns.prompt: Early signs of relief — waiting times dip. Approval ticks up.

choices.label: Invest in primary care
choices.flavour: Fix the pipeline, not just the queue.
choices.reaction: Doctors praise long-term thinking. The finance ministry updates the overdraft.

choices.label: Declare victory and move on
choices.reaction: Headlines turn favourable. The backlog moves to someone else's term.

choices.label: Expand private healthcare options
choices.reaction: Wealthy patients skip queues. Others notice the two-tier system.

turns.prompt: Credit agencies warn: borrowing costs are rising. Something has to give.

choices.label: Freeze spending across government
choices.reaction: Bond traders exhale. Public servants update their CVs.

choices.label: Borrow more and hope growth follows
choices.reaction: Investors hesitate. Consumers feel a brief boost.

choices.label: Emergency wealth tax
choices.reaction: Crowds cheer in town squares. Capital flight whispers begin.

turns.prompt: The bills from earlier choices come due. There are no good options left.

memory_callbacks.text: The hospital borrowing you approved is now crowding out everything else.
Markets remember every promise to borrow your way out.
The military cuts you made left allies questioning your commitments.

choices.label: Cut pension increases
choices.flavour: Anger retirees to placate the markets.
choices.reaction: Retirees protest outside ministries in winter coats. Bond yields fall — slightly.

choices.label: Emergency broad tax rise
choices.reaction: Competence questioned. Fiscal breathing room returns — on borrowed time.

choices.label: Let the central bank loose
choices.reaction: Short-term boom feeling. Long-term trust erodes.

turns.prompt: Final reckoning. What kind of society did you leave behind?

choices.label: Protect health at all costs
choices.flavour: Public health survives. The ledger does not.
choices.reaction: The sick get treated. The treasury gets haunted.

choices.label: Protect the economy first
choices.reaction: GDP charts smile. Waiting lists don't.

choices.label: Beg creditors for relief
choices.reaction: The ledger clears. The flag hangs a little lower.
